s: What's the most embarrassing thing you've done while trying to impress someone? Drink if you won't say.
z: What's the most embarrassing thing you've done while trying to impress someone? Do 5 squats if you won't say.
f: Do 5 squats instead.

s: Have you ever been caught snooping through someone else's stuff? Tell the story or drink.
z: Have you ever been caught snooping? Tell the story or admit what you were actually looking for.
f: Admit what you were looking for instead.

s: What's the most ridiculous reason you've had for ending something — a relationship, a friendship, a plan?
What's the worst lie you've told to get out of trouble? Drink if you skip.
z: Worst lie you've told to get out of trouble? Make up an even bigger lie on the spot if you skip.
f: Make up an even bigger lie on the spot instead.

s: What's something you've done that your parents would disown you for? Drink if you won't say.
z: What's something you've done that your parents would not approve of? Give a clue only if you won't say.
f: Give a clue only instead.

s: Have you ever drunk-texted someone and regretted it? Read the message or drink.
z: Have you ever regret-texted someone? Describe the situation without reading the message.
f: Describe the situation without reading the message instead.

s: What's the most embarrassing thing that's happened to you in front of a crowd?
What's the worst thing you've blamed on someone else — and gotten away with?
Describe your worst hangover. Be specific. Drink if you claim you've never had one.
z: Describe your worst morning-after feeling — even if it was just exhaustion. Spin in a circle if you claim you've never had one.
f: Spin in a circle if you claim you've never had one instead.

s: Have you ever talked badly about someone in this room? Confess what you said or drink.
z: Have you ever said something harsh about someone in this room? Confess the category (work, appearance, personality) without specifics.
f: Confess the category without specifics instead.

s: What's the most embarrassing reason you've unfollowed someone?
What's the pettiest thing you've ever done and are secretly proud of?
Describe the most awkward encounter you've had in a public bathroom.
What's the biggest misunderstanding you've let someone believe about you?
Have you ever been walked in on at a very bad moment? What were you doing? Drink if you won't say.
z: Have you ever been walked in on at a bad moment? What were you doing? Mime it if you won't say.
f: Mime it instead.

s: What's something you've done that you've never told anyone else in this room before?
What's the worst thing you've said about an ex — that was probably true?
Have you ever screenshotted someone's story to laugh at with others? Drink if yes.
z: Have you ever screenshotted someone's story to laugh at with others? Stand up if yes.
f: Stand up instead.

s: What's the most embarrassing thing on your internet search history right now? Drink if you won't say.
z: What's the most embarrassing thing on your internet search history? Describe the category only if you won't say.
f: Describe the category only instead.

s: What's the most ridiculous argument you've ever won — and how?
Have you ever pretended to be more experienced at something than you were? What was it?
What's the most unhinged thing you've done when you were upset?
What's the biggest thing you've ever kept secret from a close friend? Drink if you skip.
z: What's the biggest thing you've kept secret from a close friend? Describe what category it falls under if you skip.
f: Describe the category instead.

s: What's something you've done you'd be humiliated if anyone at work or school found out?
Have you ever stolen something? Even something small? Drink if you pretend you haven't.
z: Have you ever stolen something? Even something small? Do a fake innocent face if you pretend you haven't.
f: Do a fake innocent face instead.

s: What's the most embarrassing physical thing that has happened to your body at the worst possible moment?
What's something you've said out of anger that you knew wasn't true?
Have you ever made a bet you regretted? What was it? Drink if you won't say.
z: Have you ever made a bet you regretted? Describe the stakes without saying who won.
f: Describe the stakes without saying who won instead.

s: What's the most embarrassing DM you've ever sent?
Have you ever fake-laughed so hard at a joke you didn't find funny that you cried?
What's the most ridiculous reason you've ever called in sick?
Have you ever eavesdropped on a conversation and changed your behaviour based on what you heard?
What's the most embarrassing nickname you've ever been given?
What's the worst thing you've said in a voicemail?
Have you ever cried in front of a stranger? What happened? Drink if you skip.
z: Have you ever cried in front of a stranger? What happened? Mime it dramatically if you skip.
f: Mime it dramatically instead.

s: What's the most chaos you've caused at a party — not necessarily intentionally?
What's something you've accidentally ruined for someone?
Have you ever had a crush on someone wildly inappropriate — not wrong, just unexpected? Drink if you won't say.
z: Have you ever had an unexpected crush? Give a clue about them without naming them.
f: Give a clue about them instead.

s: What's the worst advice you've ever given?
What's the most dramatic exit you've made from a room?
Let the group look through your camera roll for 15 seconds — no deleting anything first.
Call someone in your contacts and sing 5 seconds of a song before hanging up.
Post an embarrassing photo of yourself from your camera roll — it must stay up for 15 minutes.
Let the person to your right write a message and send it to anyone in your contacts.
Do your most convincing impression of a wild animal, chosen by the group.
Let the group take an unflattering photo of you and set it as your phone wallpaper until the game ends.
Describe your ex using only adjectives. Be honest.
Do 20 jumping jacks while the group counts out loud.
Tell the room the most embarrassing thing about yourself that no one here knows. No backing out — if you refuse, drink.
z: Tell the room the most embarrassing thing about yourself that no one here knows. If you truly refuse, at least mime it.
f: Mime it instead if you truly can't say it.

s: Text your most recent ex and say 'Hey, how are you?' right now. Show the group.
Let the group come up with your Tinder bio. You must read it out loud exactly as written.
Show the group your most used app and your screen time total. No hiding.
Put your phone on group chat and read your most recent message thread out loud. Only one sentence.
Try to moonwalk across the room. Drink if it's terrible.
z: Try to moonwalk across the room. If it's terrible, take a bow and accept the applause anyway.
f: Take a bow and accept the applause if it's terrible instead.

s: Do an impression of every other person in the group — back to back. 10 seconds each.
Confess to one person in the room something you've thought about them that you've kept private. Keep it kind-ish.
Try to keep a straight face while the group spends 30 seconds trying to make you laugh. If you crack, drink.
z: If you crack, you have to tell us what made you break.
f: If you crack, tell us what made you break instead.

s: Allow the group to search your text messages for any contact labeled with an emoji. Read that conversation.
Freestyle rap about your most embarrassing moment. Minimum 8 bars.
Let someone in the group craft a tweet from your account. You can't read it until after it's posted. (Delete after 10 mins.)
Stand outside the room and knock. When they open it, start your best sales pitch for an imaginary product.
Describe your romantic history in exactly 5 words.
Do a handstand or headstand attempt for 10 seconds. If you can't, drink.
z: Attempt a handstand or headstand. If you can't, do 15 jumping jacks instead.
f: Do 15 jumping jacks instead.

s: Let the group rearrange your hair into the worst possible style. You wear it for 5 cards.
Do your most convincing posh British accent for the next 2 cards.
Show the group your most cringe text chain — any one from the last 3 months.
Take a selfie in the most unflattering pose possible and show everyone.
Let the group pick a story from your Instagram archive and read the caption out loud.
Try to do the worm. If you refuse, drink.
z: Try to do the worm. If you absolutely refuse, demonstrate your best other dance move.
f: Demonstrate your best other dance move instead.

s: Do your best cry-laughing impression for 15 seconds.
Let the group redecorate your social media bio for the next hour.
Go up to the nearest mirror and give yourself a motivational speech for 30 seconds.
Speak only in movie quotes for the next 3 minutes. Drink if you slip up.
z: Speak only in movie quotes for the next 3 minutes. If you slip up, explain the movie you misquoted.
f: Explain the movie you misquoted instead.

s: Let someone pick a contact in your phone. You have to text them something sweet.
Tell the group your most embarrassing drunk story in exactly 60 seconds.
Switch seats with the most fashionably dressed person in the room and compliment their style while doing so.
Open your fridge (or bag) right now and describe everything inside it in the most poetic terms possible.
Act out your most embarrassing fall or accident in slow motion.
Do an impression of how you act when you have a crush. Group must guess which crush it was based on.
Pretend to be a dog for 60 seconds. Fully commit.
Who in the room would be most likely to accidentally send a meme to their boss?
Who in the room has the most chaotic relationship with their phone?
Who in the room would cry first in a horror movie? Person with most votes takes a drink.
z: Who would cry first in a horror movie? Person with most votes must re-enact a horror movie scream.
f: Person with most votes re-enacts a horror movie scream instead.

s: Who in the room has the most questionable taste in music?
Who in the room would be most likely to accidentally cause a scene at a restaurant?
Who in the room has the most exes? Person with most votes takes a drink.
z: Who in the room has the most exes? That person must confirm or deny with a thumbs up or down.
f: Person with most votes confirms or denies with a thumbs up or down instead.

s: Who in the room would accidentally start a conspiracy theory?
Who in the room is the biggest flirt?
Who in the room would handle a break-up the worst? Most votes = drink.
z: Who would handle a break-up the worst? Most votes = confess their break-up coping mechanism.
f: Confess their break-up coping mechanism instead.

s: Who in the room is most likely to get a matching tattoo with someone after knowing them a week?
Who in the room has the most dramatic cry?
Who in the room is most likely to stay up all night over something minor? Most votes takes a drink.
z: Most votes person must tell us what minor thing they last lost sleep over.
f: Most votes person tells us what minor thing they last lost sleep over instead.

s: Who in the room would be the most extra at their own wedding?
Who in the room is most likely to accidentally overshare in a group chat?
Who in the room gives the most unsolicited opinions?
Who in the room would be the most dramatic when sick?
Who in the room is most likely to cancel plans at the last minute? Most votes = drink.
z: Most votes person must explain their most elaborate cancellation excuse.
f: Most votes person explains their most elaborate cancellation excuse instead.

s: Who in the room has the most questionable fashion history?
Who in the room would be most likely to go back to an ex?
Who in the room is the most stubborn?
Who in the room would eat fast food in a fancy restaurant if they could get away with it?
Who in the room spends the most on something the group finds frivolous? Most votes = drink.
z: Most votes person must confess what that thing is.
f: Most votes person confesses what that thing is instead.

s: Who in the room would survive a talent show?
Who in the room is most likely to start a fight over something trivial?
Who in the room is the biggest gossip? Most votes must give us a scoop right now.
Who in the room has the saddest taste in streaming content?
Who in the room would write the most chaotic diary?
Who in the room is most likely to accidentally set off an alarm?
Who in the room would send the most impulsive late-night purchase? Most votes = drink.
z: Most votes person confesses their most impulsive purchase ever.
f: Most votes person confesses their most impulsive purchase instead.

s: Who in the room has the best poker face? Group tests it: tell that person a lie and see if they flinch.
Who in the room is most likely to cry at a wedding they barely know anyone at?
Who in the room would be most likely to accidentally ruin a surprise party?
Who in the room is most likely to accidentally become famous?
Who in the room has the most embarrassing side of TikTok? Most votes = show us 10 seconds.
Who in the room would be the worst guest on a talk show?
Until the next rule card: no one can say 'like'. Anyone who does takes a drink.
z: Anyone who says 'like' must spell out the word 'inappropriate'.
f: Anyone who says 'like' spells out 'inappropriate' instead.

s: Until the next rule card: everyone must rate everything on a scale of 1-10 before speaking.
Until the next rule card: no one can say anything negative. Penalty for negativity: drink.
z: Penalty for negativity: do a compliment to the group.
f: Do a compliment to the group instead.

s: Until the next rule card: everything must be announced before doing it. 'I am now picking up my drink. I am now putting it down.' Failure: drink.
z: Failure to announce: do an interpretive move instead.
f: Do an interpretive move instead.

s: Until the next rule card: everyone must do a sound effect every time they move.
Until the next rule card: no touching your face. Penalty: drink.
z: No touching your face. Penalty: do a star jump.
f: Do a star jump instead.

s: Until the next rule card: everything must be phrased as a formal complaint. 'I formally object to this prompt...'
Until the next rule card: the first person to speak after each card must say 'plot twist...' before their response.
Until the next rule card: no one can say the word 'the'. Penalty: drink.
z: No saying 'the'. Penalty: cluck like a chicken once.
f: Cluck like a chicken once instead.

s: Until the next rule card: everything must be said at least 20% louder than normal.
Until the next rule card: every statement must be immediately questioned by someone. 'Really? Are you sure?'
Until the next rule card: no one can look anyone in the eye. Direct eye contact = drink.
z: Direct eye contact = look away dramatically and gasp.
f: Look away dramatically and gasp instead.

s: Until the next rule card: everyone must start every response with 'Allegedly...'
Until the next rule card: no one can sit down. Anyone who sits takes a drink.
z: Anyone who sits must stand and apologise dramatically.
f: Stand and apologise dramatically instead.

s: Until the next rule card: the last person to answer each question is automatically wrong. They take a drink.
z: Last person automatically wrong: they must narrate the previous person's answer better.
f: Last person narrates the previous person's answer better instead.

s: Until the next rule card: everyone must speak in past tense only, even about the present.
Until the next rule card: nothing can be said directly — everything must be a metaphor.
Until the next rule card: you must snap before speaking. If you forget, you take a drink.
z: If you forget to snap before speaking, spin around once.
f: Spin around once instead.

s: Until the next rule card: the group must unanimously vote to hear any story. The storyteller must pitch it first.
Until the next rule card: the word 'yeah' is banned. Penalty: drink.
z: The word 'yeah' is banned. Penalty: say 'indubitably' instead every time.
f: Say 'indubitably' instead every time.

s: Until the next rule card: anyone who yawns must take a drink.
z: Anyone who yawns must do 3 star jumps.
f: Do 3 star jumps instead.

s: Until the next rule card: everything must be followed by 'but that's just my opinion' even if it's clearly a fact.
Until the next rule card: everyone must clap after everything they say.
Until the next rule card: all compliments are banned. If you compliment someone, you drink.
z: All compliments banned. If you slip, you must also insult something unrelated.
f: If you slip, insult something unrelated instead.

s: Until the next rule card: the person to your right is your personal hype person and must cheer after everything you say.
Until the next rule card: no one is allowed to laugh. Penalty: drink.
z: No laughing. Penalty: do a serious-face impression for 10 seconds.
f: Do a serious-face impression for 10 seconds instead.

s: Until the next rule card: all answers must be backed up with at least one fake statistic.
Until the next rule card: the fastest person to stand up after each card is immune from any penalty.
Until the next rule card: no saying 'right', 'okay', or 'so'. Penalty: drink.
z: No 'right', 'okay', or 'so'. Penalty: spell 'exquisite' out loud.
f: Spell 'exquisite' out loud instead.

s: Until the next rule card: everyone must greet every new card with a 'Good evening, Prompt.'
Everyone who has said 'I'm almost there' while still at home — take a drink.
z: Everyone who has done this — wave your hand in the air.
f: Wave your hand in the air instead.

s: Everyone who has gone back for a second helping and pretended it was their first — drink.
z: Everyone who has done this — raise your hand sheepishly.
f: Raise your hand sheepishly instead.

s: Everyone who has accidentally called a teacher 'mum' or 'dad' — drink.
z: Everyone who has done this — re-enact the moment.
f: Re-enact the moment instead.

s: Everyone who has stalked someone on social media for longer than 30 minutes — drink.
z: Everyone who has done this — say 'I have no regrets'.
f: Say 'I have no regrets' instead.

s: Everyone who has ended a call and then immediately said something rude not realising they weren't hung up — drink.
z: Everyone who has done this — re-enact the aftermath face.
f: Re-enact the aftermath face instead.

s: Everyone who has lied about their age — drink.
z: Everyone who has done this — tell us which direction and why.
f: Tell us which direction (up or down) and why instead.

s: Everyone who has faked their location on Snapchat or Life360 — drink.
z: Everyone who has done this — tell us where you actually were.
f: Tell us where you actually were instead.

s: Everyone who has sent a complaining text about someone to that person by mistake — drink.
z: Everyone who has done this — describe what happened next.
f: Describe what happened next instead.

s: Everyone who has rehearsed a conversation before having it — drink.
z: Everyone who has done this — demonstrate your rehearsed opening line.
f: Demonstrate your rehearsed opening line instead.

s: Everyone who has subtweeted or vague-posted about someone — drink.
z: Everyone who has done this — confess who it was about (category is fine).
f: Confess who it was about (category is fine) instead.

s: Everyone who has turned down plans and then been spotted out — drink.
z: Everyone who has done this — recreate the face you made when spotted.
f: Recreate the face you made when spotted instead.

s: Everyone who has gone back to check if the door is locked more than twice — drink.
z: Everyone who has done this — do a 'did I lock it?' dramatic check mime.
f: Do a 'did I lock it?' dramatic check mime instead.

s: Everyone who has eaten someone else's food in the fridge without asking — drink.
z: Everyone who has done this — apologise to no one in particular.
f: Apologise to no one in particular instead.

s: Everyone who has added people they barely know as character references — drink.
z: Everyone who has done this — give a 5-second version of what you'd hope they said.
f: Give a 5-second version of what you'd hope they said instead.

s: Everyone who has cried in a car so no one else could see — drink.
z: Everyone who has done this — give a moment of silence.
f: Give a moment of silence instead.

s: Everyone who has been the cause of a miscommunication that went on too long — drink.
z: Everyone who has done this — admit how long it went on.
f: Admit how long it went on instead.

s: Everyone who has muted someone on social media without unfollowing — drink.
z: Everyone who has done this — raise a hand.
f: Raise a hand instead.

s: Everyone who has eaten an entire meal standing over the sink — drink.
z: Everyone who has done this — own it with a head nod.
f: Own it with a head nod instead.

s: Everyone who has screenshot-shared a message someone sent them — drink.
z: Everyone who has done this — say 'not my finest hour'.
f: Say 'not my finest hour' instead.

s: Everyone who has lied about what they were doing when someone called — drink.
z: Everyone who has done this — mime what you were actually doing.
f: Mime what you were actually doing instead.

s: Everyone who has cried watching a competition reality show — drink.
z: Everyone who has done this — name the show and the moment.
f: Name the show and the moment instead.

s: Everyone who has Googled themselves — drink.
z: Everyone who has done this — tell us what you found (or didn't find).
f: Tell us what you found (or didn't find) instead.

s: Everyone who has bought something on impulse and hidden it from a partner or family member — drink.
z: Everyone who has done this — describe what the item was in vague terms.
f: Describe the item in vague terms instead.

s: Everyone who has told someone off mentally in the shower and felt great about it — drink.
z: Everyone who has done this — give us 5 seconds of the shower speech.
f: Give us 5 seconds of the shower speech instead.

s: Everyone who has eaten food off the floor of their own home without any hesitation — drink.
z: Everyone who has done this — say 'my house, my rules'.
f: Say 'my house, my rules' instead.

s: {P} — pick someone in the room whose contact name in your phone is a nickname. Show them without showing anyone else.
{P} — call out the person in the room you'd trust least with a secret. They take a drink.
z: The called-out person must explain why they think they earned that reputation.
f: The called-out person explains why they earned that reputation instead.

s: {P} — describe {P2}'s worst habit using only mime. {P2} guesses what it is.
{P1} and {P2} — you have 60 seconds to write a parody song about each other. Perform it.
{P} — rank everyone in the room from most to least likely to be the last one standing at a party. The person ranked last takes a drink.
z: Person ranked last tells us why {P} is wrong.
f: Person ranked last tells us why {P} is wrong instead.

s: {P} — let {P2} go through your DMs for 20 seconds. They can't share what they see.
{P} — rate everyone's outfit tonight from 1-10. The lowest score takes a drink.
z: Lowest score must explain to {P} why they're wrong about fashion.
f: Lowest score explains to {P} why they're wrong instead.

s: {P1} and {P2} — try to do the same dance move at the same time without planning. If you fail, both drink.
z: If you fail, both must do 5 jumping jacks.
f: Both do 5 jumping jacks if they fail instead.

s: {P} — tell {P2} the most honest opinion you've kept to yourself about them. Keep it respectful but real.
{P} — guess {P2}'s most-played song. If wrong, {P} takes a drink.
z: If wrong, {P} must do a freestyle dance for 10 seconds.
f: If wrong, freestyle dance for 10 seconds instead.

s: {P} — pick the person you think is hardest to read. They must answer the next truth card with no lying.
{P1} — do your best impression of how {P2} acts when they're trying to seem cool. {P2} rates it.
{P} — pick the person who you think has the most interesting secret right now. They must confirm or deny (without revealing the secret) by taking a drink.
z: They confirm or deny with a thumbs up or down instead.
f: Confirm or deny with thumbs up or down instead.

s: {P} — allow {P2} to tell the group what they really think you're like when no one's watching. {P} rates the accuracy.
{P} — swap phones with {P2} for 2 cards. No looking at anything.
{P} — summarise {P2}'s dating history as if it were a Netflix show, including the seasons and the finale.
{P1} and {P2} — you must argue opposite sides of which is better: summer or winter. Group votes the winner. Loser drinks.
z: Loser must admit one thing the winner's choice is actually better at.
f: Loser admits one thing the winner's choice is actually better at instead.

s: {P} — confess something you've borrowed from {P2} and never returned (or someone like them).
{P} — describe {P2} to the room as if they're a character in a soap opera. What's their dramatic storyline?
{P} — pick the person in the room you'd least trust to plan a surprise. That person must explain why they'd actually be great at it.
{P1} — ask {P2} a question you've always wanted to ask but never have. {P2} must answer honestly or take a drink.
z: {P2} must answer honestly or do 5 jumping jacks.
f: {P2} does 5 jumping jacks instead.

s: {P} — describe the funniest misunderstanding you've had with someone in this room. They confirm.
{P} — tell {P2} one thing they do that secretly drives you a bit crazy. Keep it affectionate.
{P} — pick who in the room has changed the most since you first met them. That person takes a drink and says if they agree.
z: That person says whether they agree and gives a thumbs up or down.
f: That person says whether they agree with a thumbs up or down instead.

s: {P} — do your best impression of how {P2} acts when they haven't had their morning coffee. {P2} rates it.
{P1} and {P2} — argue about the best meal for breakfast. The group votes for a winner. Loser makes a dramatic concession speech.
{P} — pick the person in the room most likely to start a fight over something small. That person must react calmly to prove you wrong.
{P} — guess {P2}'s most recent Google search. If right, {P2} drinks. If wrong, {P} drinks.
z: If right, {P2} does 5 squats. If wrong, {P} does 5 squats.
f: If right {P2} does 5 squats, if wrong {P} does 5 squats instead.

s: {P} — tell the group the most ridiculous story about {P2} that is 100% true. {P2} confirms or corrects.
{P1} — describe {P2} using only the titles of TV shows. Group judges if they're accurate.
